summary: I was born and raised in Somalia, specifically in Mogadishu, where I grew up in a close-knit neighborhood. My father served in the military until 1991, when the Somali Civil War began and the government started to collapse. Many of my relatives were killed during the war, which forced me and my son to leave Somalia in 2002 to seek safety. Somalia is my homeland, and although there is now more peace, I hope to return someday to visit.

After fleeing Somalia in 2002, my son and I went to Egypt because I had a friend there. We lived in Cairo, but life was difficult since I struggled to find work and afford education for my children. My daughter was born in Egypt during this time, and despite the challenges, we tried to live peacefully. Eventually, I applied to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees for resettlement from Egypt.

entity: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
summary: I reached out to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees while living in Egypt because I needed help securing a better future for my family. They have been assisting refugees worldwide for over fifty years, and after a four-year wait, they accepted me and my children as refugees. Thanks to their support, we were resettled in the United States, which gave me hope for a new beginning despite the struggles I faced upon arrival.

I was resettled in the United States after being accepted as a refugee by the UNHCR. Living in St. Paul, Minnesota, I faced many challenges such as not speaking English and having no money when I first arrived. I work hard, study English, and raise my children here, hoping to build a better future for them. The United States became my new home after years of hardship and waiting.

The Somali Civil War started in 1991, the same year my father stopped serving in the military. It was a devastating time when the government collapsed and people began fighting each other, destroying property and taking many lives, including those of my uncle and other relatives. This war forced me to flee Somalia with my son in 2002 to save our lives. The conflict shaped much of my early life and the difficult journey that followed.

I was born in Mogadishu in December and grew up in a beautiful neighborhood where everyone knew each other. As a child, I visited my great-grandfather who had many animals, which was an exciting experience for me. Mogadishu was my home until the civil war began in 1991, which eventually forced me to leave for safety reasons. Despite the hardships, I have fond memories of my early years in Mogadishu.

Now I live in St. Paul, Minnesota, where I work, study English, and raise my children. Life here is still a struggle because I have to balance school and a second-shift job, but I am determined to provide a good future for my family. St. Paul became my home after resettlement from Egypt through the UNHCR, and I am grateful for the opportunities it offers. I wake up early every day to prepare my children for school and keep pushing forward.
